queries: LLM tool-use chain cascade information narrowing tool selection ReAct multi-tool reasoning 2025
multi-agent LLM debate self-consistency cross-check tool 3-agent agreement termination 2025

raw_responses.title: The Evolution of Tool Use in LLM Agents: From Single-Tool Call to Multi-Tool Orchestration
raw_responses.snippet: Tool space expands to thousands of APIs with non-linear dependencies; autoregressive generation struggles with cascading errors. Direct discussion of multi-tool cascades.

raw_responses.title: Chain-of-Tools (CoTools)
raw_responses.snippet: Frozen LLM dynamically interacts with tools; learns when to use tool and which to select. Chain-of-tools = cascade.

raw_responses.title: Robust and Efficient Tool Orchestration via Layered Execution Structures with Reflective Correction
raw_responses.snippet: Layered execution + reflective correction. Cascade with self-correction at each layer; analog to scholarly cross-check.

raw_responses.title: ToolRLA: Multiplicative Reward Decomposition for Tool-Integrated Agents
raw_responses.snippet: Multiplicative reward across tool calls; downstream tool's reward decomposes upstream choices.

raw_responses.title: Multi-Agent Debate for LLM Judges with Adaptive Stability Detection
raw_responses.snippet: 3-agent CollabEval framework: independent assessment → consensus check → final results when consensus reached. Direct functional analog of Behistun 4-scholar blind verification.

raw_responses.title: Improving Factuality and Reasoning with Multiagent Debate
raw_responses.snippet: Multiple LLM instances debate; consensus-based termination. Foundational multi-agent debate paper.

raw_responses.title: Adaptive heterogeneous multi-agent debate
raw_responses.snippet: Heterogeneous 2025 debate framework with adaptive stability/agreement detection for termination.

raw_responses.title: Multi-Agent Debate for LLM Judges with Adaptive Stability Detection (arxiv)
raw_responses.snippet: Same framework as OpenReview; arxiv version. Adaptive stability detection = analog of 'when do you stop the debate' = 'when do the 3 scholars' translations agree'.
